item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the original source cannot be found and no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
two spelling/grammar fixes in the addendum. Changed

a 18

to

an 18

and

girls' note

to

girl's note

(there's only one girl, right?)

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Ah yes, another Alexvya University skip. Until at such a time that an Alexvya GoI hub has been created, the tale series Parable of the Wayward Prince is really the closest thing that this GoI has to a hub, and linking from this skip to there is a good way of ensuring that those who like this skip can find other related material as well as the internal story of the Foundation's interactions with AU. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

From the personal effects of the two former possessors of SCP-1546, letters were located. Both had identical letterheads reading "ALEXYLVA UNIVERSITY—DEPARTMENT OF NATURAL PHILOSOPHY" and handwritten notes on them. The girls' note contained several mathematical formulas similar to the work produced by Albert Einstein and Nathan Rosen on the subject of space-time bridges, though the formulae rely on slightly different mathematical constants and operators than those typically used in such work.

To this:

From the personal effects of the two former possessors of SCP-1546, letters were located. Both had identical letterheads reading "ALEXYLVA UNIVERSITY—DEPARTMENT OF NATURAL PHILOSOPHY" and handwritten notes on them. The girls' note contained several mathematical formulas similar to the work produced by Albert Einstein and Nathan Rosen on the subject of space-time bridges, though the formulae rely on slightly different mathematical constants and operators than those typically used in such work.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
blah blah blah magical item

This is boring, downvo-

WE GIVE TO OUR KIDS AS TOYS.

…oh dear.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have to say, my initial reaction was that this was too bland an item and had a pretty standard effect that I didn't think was terribly interesting.

Then I read the note, and upvoted immediately. The development of the Alexylva plot is very welcome, and gives the 'bland' item a far more menacing aspect (the hint of just how widespread this is in Alexylva's reality, and how this isn't at all the worst they can do).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is well-written, but that doesn't stop it from being bland, bland, bland. The writing isn't good enough to overcome that (unlike, say, SCP-772).

I like the University stuff, though, and that's the only thing that's preventing me from downvoting. Still, this is the second bland SCP item I've seen with interesting University stuff attached to it. That is by no means a good trend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The item is pretty bland, though it certainly doesn't need a horrible side effect. That said, I quite like Alexylva University. They're surprisingly menacing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was me. Just because a concept can be glibly summarized in three words, doesn't make it a bad idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's very, VERY important that this chip is researched.
Broadcasting on that frequency with an antenna this short is really not something modern tech can do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Part of why I wrote it was to come up with an item that is very useful, but only if you are an asshole. Therefore pretty much anyone thinking "that'd be useful to have" is basically saying "Gosh, I wish I could trick people into liking me!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking magnets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well there's something I've been meaning to tell you
about the college on the edge of the world
No one should ever go there
You know it's bad, bad, bad
It gets worse every school year
but man the freakin teachers are raaaddd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My main issue with this article is the lack of a bad side effect. Sure, someone killed herself out of guilt. So what if an asshole took the cap? Assholes are jaded from the effects of manipulating people or watching people fail because of them. It's just missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, as VAE pointed out, http://www.newscientist.com/article/mg19025456.500-device-warns-you-if-youre-boring-or-irritating.html

There are glasses that do a similar sort of thing, reading facial expressions and telling you some of what the other person is thinking. Not as advanced, of course, but the idea does exist here, now, today. So it's not magic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, so ████████ University finally gets a name. Pretty good hat, though I feel like something similar has been done multiple times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alexylva University is now my absolute favorite thing on the internet, so when i saw a new SCP related to it i was excited, the i got to the end and was pleased to see that the story is moving forward, just one complaint, i suppose Hailcloud could be lying, but no matter how advanced a civilization giving this to children can't possibly be a good idea. also, does the University make up the entire of that universe's society or are there other things "over there"?

Edit: why a "W" wouldnt a greek letter or an "A" or something make more sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not as put off by the idea as many here are, making every item of potential usefulness dangerous to its user makes things too standardized in other ways. But that said, that is the first thing that came to mind when I finished reading this, and felt obligated to point out the elephant in the room. Not voting, giving you time to make possible adjustments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted briefly, but then I reconsidered it. I guess I forgot to remove it. Thanks for the heads up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't realized that it was phrased like that, but what I meant to say was "the mother found this note, the police logged it as curious or someone else wrote something about it, the Foundation picked up on it." Might rephrase if that's a bigger problem than I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess I just don't understand why the Foundation would search through the personal effects of a random girl who committed suicide and left a rather normal suicide note. What did she do to gain their attention?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The user as in the person wearing the hat? Lemme see what I can do.

Also, in the interest of full disclosure, it says on the page that you upvoted. Not complaining, just making sure you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This could be better with some sort of appropriate explicit mental effect on the user, in my opinion. On the whole, though, I don't have a strong enough opinion on it either way to vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was worried it would be taken that way. It's handwaved technology, but it's still technology. If you figure what cell phone technology has done in the last twenty years, the capacity for this might not even be beyond our eventual development.

But if you think it sucks, there isn't really any fixing that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Straight up magic item. No thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I swear to god that the phrase "cap of charisma" never entered my head until someone mentioned it in chat. I almost drowned this thing in the sandbox at that very moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +5 CHA Cap of Persuasion
